title: llm-tokens-atlas dataset schema
description: Schema (draft 2020-12) for the three JSONL streams that compose llm-tokens-atlas: 'prompts' (data/raw_prompts.jsonl), 'offline_counts' (data/offline_counts.jsonl), and 'empirical_counts' (data/empirical_counts.jsonl). The downstream join key is (prompt_id, provider, format, model). Schema version 1: backwards-compatible additions allowed; breaking changes bump to v2.json.

format.description: Prompt serialization format. One of five enum values (lowercased).

provider.description: LLM provider whose tokenizer (offline) or token-count endpoint (empirical) produced the count.

prompt_id.description: Stable identifier for a single prompt row in raw_prompts.jsonl. Used as the foreign key in offline_counts and empirical_counts.

nonNegativeInteger.description: A token count or character length, never negative.

promptRow.description: One row of data/raw_prompts.jsonl. The canonical source-of-truth text + provenance for a single prompt.

text.description: The prompt text exactly as it will be sent to the tokenizer / API. UTF-8.

text_len_chars.description: Length of 'text' in Unicode codepoints (not bytes).

text_len_words.description: Whitespace-split word count of 'text'. Approximate — intended for filtering/grouping, not as a tokenization signal.

language.description: ISO-639-1 (or BCP-47) language code: 'en', 'zh', 'es', 'fr', 'de', 'ja', 'multi', 'code', etc. 'code' indicates source code; 'multi' indicates a mixed-language prompt.

domain.description: High-level domain tag used for stratified analysis.

offlineCountRow.description: One row of data/offline_counts.jsonl. Offline-tokenizer count for a single (prompt_id, provider, format, model) tuple. No network call; pure local tokenization via @tokenometer/core (or the moral equivalent).

offline_count.description: Token count reported by the offline tokenizer. Never negative.

tokenizer_version.description: Pinned tokenizer version identifier (e.g. 'tiktoken@cl100k_base', '@tokenometer/core@1.0.0', 'mistral-common@1.7.0'). Pinned for reproducibility.

empiricalCountRow.description: One row of data/empirical_counts.jsonl. Ground-truth token count for a single (prompt_id, provider, format, model) tuple. Sourced from each provider's official countTokens endpoint or (where no endpoint exists) tiktoken-as-truth.

empirical_count.description: Token count reported by the provider's count-tokens endpoint or by tiktoken (when treated as oracle, e.g. for OpenAI).

is_oracle.description: True when this value is treated as the ground-truth oracle (e.g. tiktoken for OpenAI; provider countTokens API for Anthropic/Google). False when it is an empirical-but-not-official source (e.g. inferred from stream usage).

source.description: How this empirical count was obtained: 'api' = HTTP call to a countTokens endpoint; 'tiktoken' = local tiktoken treated as oracle (OpenAI only); 'sdk' = vendor SDK helper; 'stream-usage' = inferred from generation usage metadata.

endpoint.description: Concrete endpoint or library identifier — e.g. 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages/count_tokens@2024-10-22', 'vertex-ai.googleapis.com/v1/.../countTokens', 'tiktoken.encoding_for_model(gpt-4o)'.

tables.description: Index of the three tables in the atlas dataset. Each table.row is a $ref into $defs and identifies the per-row schema used to validate the corresponding JSONL stream.

join.description: Documented downstream join semantics. scripts/build_dataset.py inner-joins offline_counts and empirical_counts onto prompts using (prompt_id, provider, format, model) to produce the processed Parquet artifact.
